observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:

a. Municipal General Services Officer to facilitate the reconciliation of the RPCPPE
and the Property Cards; and

b. Municipal Accountant to conduct a comprehensive review of all derecognized
unserviceable properties and record all PPE accounts which are not yet disposed
of to ensure proper compliance with the disposal procedures under Sections 194
and 195 of the COA Circular No. 92-386.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Determine the useful life and amortization method to be used, and adjust the
carrying amount of the Intangible Assets based on the computed accumulated
amortization from December 2022 to 2024; and

b. Provide the required disclosures for the Intangible Assets in accordance with
IPSAS 31.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
provide computation and draw Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) for the recognition of
depreciation expense on the PPE accounts with a total gross amount of
P6,943,691.00.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and
Municipal Accountant to use the OMOE account only for expenditures that cannot be
classified under specific expense accounts in accordance with IPSAS 1, COA Circular
No. 2015-009 and Section 4 of COA Circular No. 2024-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the:

a. Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and maintain the SPC, RegSPI, and
RSPI, for the establishment of proper control and accountability over the semi-
expendable property; and

b. Municipal Accountant to maintain the SPLC to record the acquisition, description,
custody, impairment, issue/transfer/disposal, repair history, and other information
about the property.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
formulate and adopt more aggressive collection strategies; otherwise, consider
availing the remedies provided under Sections 176 and 256 of RA No. 7160 to enforce
the collection and reduction of delinquent real property taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Human Resource Management
Officer to prepare a contract, MOA, or job order between the Municipality of Majayjay
and each JO worker to establish a clear and binding agreement and ensure that
comprehensive provisions, including but not limited to the workers’ duties,
responsibilities, terms of employment, payment structure, and the agreed method of
payment (daily wage or piece of work) were stated in the contract to facilitate the
proper validation of workers performance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Development Council,
through the MPDC and in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer to:

a. Ensure that the planned PPAs under the 20 percent DF are well-planned and
procurement- and implementation-ready; and

b. Identify the specific PPAs to be funded from the 20 percent DF and refrain from
the inclusion of lump-sum or generic terms to ensure the optimal utilization of
the fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Council, through the MDRRMO to:

a. Include and present under a separate caption the list of specific PPAs to be funded
from the previous years’ unexpended LDRRMF, indicating therein the specific
funding source of the year of transfer of each PPA in the LDRRMFIP, following Item
5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002; and

b. Adequately plan and monitor the PPAs included in the LDRRMFIP to ensure the
effective utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer to refrain from incurring and charging expenses
which do not fall within the four thematic areas of Disaster Risk Reduction and
Management.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Inventory Committee to assign a unique property number for all the PPE
accounts with complete vital information;
b. Municipal General Services Officer to prepare and maintain Property Cards for
all PPE accounts and Property Acknowledgement Receipts upon issuance of
PPE and ensure its renewal every three years or every time there is a change
in accountability or custodianships;
c. Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to undertake
collaborative procedures to reconcile both records; and
d. Municipal General Services Officer, Municipal Accountant and the Inventory
Committee to ensure full and timely implementation of the cleansing process
as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2020-006.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to transfer the Special Trust Fund to the Trust Fund books
and to transfer only the unexpended/unobligated balance of the Quick
Response Fund and DRRMF-Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses from
the General Fund to the Special Trust Fund at year-end;
b. Municipal Accountant to correct the erroneous recording of the unexpended
balance of the Calendar Year 2023 LDRRMF for Capital Outlay and Continuing
Appropriation totaling P5,766,548.12 and unappropriated amount totaling
P1,509,288.67 as Trust Liabilities-DRRMF, the balances should be retained in
the General Fund as Continuing Appropriation and Unappropriated surplus,
respectively; and
c. Ensure proper accounting and recording for the LDRRMF, in compliance with
Item 5.1 and Annex C of COA Circular No. 2012-002 and Annex B of COA
Circular No. 2015-009 for the fair presentation of financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Record construction project under CIP account based on the actual
accomplishment and recognize liability only when the goods and services are
accepted or rendered and supplier/creditor bills are received, in compliance
with Section 4(s) of MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b. Present the unperformed/unbilled portion of the construction project which
was already fully obligated during the year under “timing difference” of the
reconciliation between actual amounts on a comparable basis in the Statement
of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts and the Statement of Financial
Performance as presented in the Notes to Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare Schedule of Unreleased Checks monthly based
on the actual release or issuance to the payees and forward the same to the
Municipal Accountant, in compliance with Section 2.3 of GAFMIS Circular
Letter No. 2002-001; and
b. Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher at year-end to record
the restoration of unreleased checks and recognize the appropriate liability
accounts to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the
financial statements, in compliance with Section 2.4 of GAFMIS Circular Letter
No. 2002-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to address the difference in the
accounts Due to/from LGUs and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that
the balances of the accounts are in agreement. Moreover, regularly furnish the
Provincial Accountant with the copy of Journal Entry Voucher and/or summary of
collections, as basis in recording in the books of the Municipality and Province,
respectively.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. Review the previous years' books of accounts to ascertain the transaction year
of the Due from Officers and Employees totaling P79,841.00 and gather
relevant information and documents to validate its existence; and
b. Conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis and validation of the
existence of the receivables, and determine the concerned debtors in
compliance with Item 7.1 of COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. Disbursing officer to submit the liquidation reports for cash advances for the
wages of JO Workers to the Municipal Accountant within five days after each
15-day/monthly pay period;
b. Municipal Accountant to strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances for
wages of JO workers within the prescribed period, in accordance Item 5.1.1 of
COA Circular No. 97-002; and
c. Human Resource Management Officer and Municipal Treasurer to coordinate
with the government bank for the prompt enrollment of all JO workers in
Automated Teller Machine payroll accounts to strengthen the internal control
of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to stop
the granting of cash advances to AOs in excess of their maximum cash
accountability, otherwise increase the fidelity bond of AOs sufficient to cover their
accountabilities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct Local Development Council,
through the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and in coordination
with the Municipal Budget Officer, to clearly specify PPAs to be funded from the
20 Percent DF and to refrain from including lump-sum appropriations in the
Annual Investment Plan to ensure optimal utilization of the appropriation.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local
Disaster Risk Reduction Management Council through the Municipal Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer, in coordination with other concerned officials to:
a. Effectively plan Programs, Projects and Activities for the LDRRMF and include
all of the available funds in the LDRRMF Investment Plan, as incorporated in
the local development plan, annual work and financial plan; and
b. Ensure the optimal utilization of the LDRRMF by closely monitoring its
implementation and prioritize the utilization of unexpended balances from
prior years to make sure that these funds are used within the prescribed
period.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to enforce collection of the delinquent real property taxes by
conducting tax campaign and granting incentives or relief to taxpayers thru
compromise agreement, with the authority of the Sangguniang Bayan, otherwise,
avail of the remedies provided under Section 256 of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the GAD Focal Point System,
through the GAD Focal Person to ensure proper attribution of projects's annual
expenditure to the GAD expenditures based on the result of the Harmonized
Gender and Development Guidelines tool, in accordance with Item 4.1.C.4 of PCW-
DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01, amended by Item 3 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA
JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Budget Officer to:
a. Maintain RAAOCO and its separate registries for current and continuing
appropriations, in accordance with the prescribed format in Sections 18 and 19
of Chapter I of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II, for proper monitoring of
appropriation and utilization of the 20 percent DF; and
b. Present actual expenditures and utilized amounts by specific appropriations in
the SCBAA and Utilization Report to verify utilization status and ensure report
reliability.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Human Resource
Management Officer to consider creating the position required for the head of the
LDRRMO, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer, in compliance with
Section 12(b) of RA No. 10121 and item 5 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No.
2014-1, and to appoint qualified personnel based on the qualification standards
set in item 6 of the JMC.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. gather relative supporting documents and establish the breakdown of the Due from Officers and Employees totaling P79,841.00; and b. prepare and maintain Subsidiary Ledgers for each debtor based on the supporting documents gathered in compliance with Section 10 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II and Sections No. 112 and 114 of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: Details of the Due from Officers and Employees totaling P79,841.00; and Subsidiary Ledgers for each debtor were not completed due to lack of proper turnover between the former and incumbent Municipal Accountant.
observations.reason: Incomplete records due to lack of proper turnover between the former and incumbent Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Bids and Awards Committee Secretariat to post the 28 procurement projects totaling P9,703,085.65 undertaken through Emergency Procurement under the Bayanihan Act and the updated APP in the GPPB Online Portal, in promotion of compliance with the pertinent provisions of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020 and GPPB TSO Advisory Nos. 04-2020 and 05-2020.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is now knowledgeable on the process of consultancy services procurement.
observations.reason: The BAC Secretariat is not aware of the GPPB Online Portal.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal General Services Officer and Municipal Accountant to conduct
periodic reconciliation of their records and make the necessary adjustments in
the books of accounts or reports, if warranted;
b. Municipal Accountant to identify the details of the beginning balances of PPEs
totaling P3,013,945.03 by tracing source documents;
c. Municipal General Services Officer to maintain Property Card for each class of
PPE, in compliance with Section 114 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
d. Inventory Committee to conduct a complete inventory count and prepare a fully
accomplished RPCPPE, in accordance with Section 124 of the MNGAS for LGUs,
Volume I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. reclassify the outstanding cash advances totaling P472,930.00 erroneously
recorded as Advances to Operating Expenses account to Advances to Special
Disbursing Officer account;
b. demand the immediate liquidation of outstanding cash advances totaling
P5,785,682.51;
c. reclassify the outstanding cash advance of the former Municipal Treasurer from
Advances to Special Disbursing Officer account to Other Receivables account;
d. send demand letter to the former Municipal Treasurer requiring the refund of
her unliquidated cash advances amounting to P10,000.00;
e. ensure that officials and employees are required to secure clearance from
money and property accountabilities before resignation, retirement, transfer or
dismissal from office; and
f. strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period
and ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year
pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. identify the cause of the discrepancy of P1,147,928.35 between the balances
reported in the Statement of Financial Position and NTFS and make necessary
adjustments; and

b. fully disclose the details of the previous years’ unexpended balances of
LDRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements, as required under Item 5 of COA
Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to draw Journal Entry Voucher debiting RPT/SET
Receivable and crediting Deferred RPT/SET to include the unrecorded
delinquent taxes totaling P2,408,944.24; and

b. Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant a duly signed certified
list of taxpayers and the amount due and collectible at the beginning of the year
so that the latter will have a basis in setting up the RPT and SET Receivables of
the Municipality, in accordance with Section 20 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume
I.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and
Municipal Treasurer to:

a. identify the causes of the noted discrepancies and immediately make necessary
adjustments on the noted discrepancies between their records; and

b. conduct regular reconciliation of records to ensure that any discrepancies could
immediately be identified and corrected, in accordance with Sections 123 and
124 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal General
Services Officer to:

a. furnish the Office of the Auditor with a copy of PO, irrespective of amount,
and each of every supporting documents, within five working days from the
issuance thereof, in compliance with Item 3.2.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-
001; and

b. notify the Office of the Auditor of the time and date of every scheduled
delivery of procured items within 24 hours from its acceptance, in
compliance with Item A(2) of COA Circular No. 96-010.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Designated Municipal
General Services Officer to:

a. complete the lacking information from the noted 257 POs to avoid the
issuance of Notice of Suspension; and

b. adhere with Item B of COA Circular No. 96-010 on the preparation of PO
complete with all the necessary information pertaining to the procurement
to ensure that the supplier will deliver the items in accordance with the terms
and conditions of the contract.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. submit the required lacking documents complete or supplied with necessary
information needed to support the noted emergency procurement to avoid the
issuance of Notice of Suspension; and

b. ensure that all procurements undertaken through Negotiated Procurement –
Emergency Cases are supported with complete documentation, in compliance with
Section 4(6) of PD No. 1445 and Item 4 of GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

a. Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized balances of fund transfers to the
respective source agencies or to National Treasury, in accordance with Item 4.9
of COA Circular No. 94-013; and

b. Municipal Accountant to submit to the Office of the Auditor copy of the
corresponding liquidation reports and other supporting documents thereto.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a. require the concerned personnel to submit the lacking documents presented in
Appendix H and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor to support the total
amounts claimed and to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension on the
noted transactions; and

b. ensure that the payments of COVID-19 SRA to PHWs are supported with
complete documentation, in compliance with the pertinent provisions of COA
Circular No. 2012-001 and DBM and DOH JC Nos. 2020-2 and 2021-01.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Planning and
Development Coordinator and OIC-Municipal Budget Officer to:

a. submit a written justification as to the reason of the charging of the noted
expenditures totaling P2,139,403.92 against the 20 per cent Development Fund;
and

b. refrain from charging unallowable expenditures against the 20 per cent
Development Fund and strategically plan the specific PPAs which are essential
to the promotion of the welfare of the people, in accordance with DBM, DOF and
DILG JMC No. 1, series of 2020.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the Inventory Committee to complete the inventory count to prove the existence of all of the property and submit to the Audit Team the complete Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE); b. create an Appraisal Committee to assess the current fair value of the donated property without cost; and c. require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal General Services Officer to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records and make the necessary corrections or adjustments on the discrepancy between the accounting and property records.
observations.managementAction: Appraisal Committee was already created by the Municipality.
observations.reason: RPCPPE was only limited to Land, Road Networks, Buildings, Office Equipment and Motor Vehicles since Inventory Committee had limited data on all of the property of Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to: a. strictly enforce the liquidation of cash advances within the prescribed period and ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at the end of each year pursuant to the pertinent provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002; b. send demand letter to the former Municipal Treasurer requiring the refund of her unliquidated cash advance amounting to P10,000.00; and c. reclassify the outstanding cash advance of the former Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant agreed to comply with the audit recommendations and committed to effect the necessary adjustments in the books, ensure immediate liquidations of the cash advances by strictly requiring the accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Outstanding cash advance of the former Municipal Treasurer was still not yet refunded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to provide sufficient disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.managementAction: Details of the LDRRMF were already disclosed in the Notes to Financial Statements in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant did not follow the required format of the disclosure. It was only limited to the aggregate amounts of appropriation, utilization and unutilized balance.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Provincial Accountant to address the difference in the accounts Due to/from LGUs and make the necessary adjustments to ensure that the balances of the accounts are in agreement. Moreover, regularly furnish the Provincial Accountant with the copy of Journal Entry Voucher and/or summary of collections, as basis in recording in the books of the Municipality and Province, respectively.
observations.managementAction: The reported balances of Due to and Due from LGUs accounts related to the transactions with the Province of Laguna are now reconciled with the books of the Provincial Government of Laguna.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: a. record the cash donations received under the Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account and maintain separate subsidiary ledger for cash donations received by the Municipality per donor per purpose and those below P100,000.00, which shall be grouped and labeled as Various Donors per purpose, as required in Item IV.B of COA Circular 2014-002; and b. adjust the unutilized balance of the donations amounting to P60,000.00 from Trust Liabilities to Trust Liabilities - DRRMF account and remit any unutilized balance to the Bureau of the Treasury (BTr) as soon as the COVID-19 pandemic ends.
observations.managementAction: A separate subsidiary ledger is now being maintained. JEV No. GJ-TF-2021-06-069 dated 6/28/2021 was already drawn to adjust the unutilized cash donations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to remit the subsidy returned by six beneficiaries totaling P39,000.00 to the DSWD; and b. Municipal Accountant to adjust the erroneous balances of the affected entries previously made by debiting the Prior Period Adjustment and crediting Due to NGAs accounts both by P39,000.00.
observations.managementAction: Cash subsidies returned by six beneficiaries totaling P39,000.00 were already remitted to the DSWD. Necessary adjusting entries to correct the erroneous balances of the affected accounts were already drawn by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and other concerned personnel to immediately submit all the documents needed (Appendix M) to support the emergency procurement under the Bayanihan Act as required under Section 4 (6) of PD No. 1445 and GPPB Circular No. 01-2020.
observations.managementAction: The noted lacking supporting documents were already secured by the Municipal Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant explained that they are still reviewing the documents.
observations.reason: The Office of the Auditor is yet to receive the noted lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) and the Special Disbursing Officer to explain in writing the lapses such as: • delays in the distribution of SAP-ESP; • allowing the non-submission of IDs of some beneficiaries; and • inclusion of unqualified beneficiaries in the final list of SAP-ESP recipients.
observations.managementAction: The MSWDO submitted a written justification regarding the noted lapses. Upon further validation, the Audit Team accepted the justification.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant and all the concerned personnel to immediately submit the documents needed (Appendix M) to support the disbursements for the three infrastructure projects.
observations.managementAction: The noted lacking supporting documents were already secured by the Municipal Accounting Office. The Municipal Accountant explained that they are still reviewing the documents.
observations.reason: The Office of the Auditor is yet to receive the noted lacking documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Budget Officer to revert to the 20 per cent Development Fund the expenditures that do not cost incurred for the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes and, in addition, refrain from charging expenditures that do not partake the nature of investments or capital expenditures and are not related to the attainment of socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes against the 20 per cent development fund.
observations.managementAction: The expenses noted contribute to the general welfare of the residents of the Municipality. Adherence with the guidelines on the utilization of the 20 per cent Development Fund is now being observed by the Municipality.
observations.reason: The Municipality explained that the expenses noted contribute to the general welfare of the residents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. instruct the Bids and Awards Committee to conduct public bidding in the procurement of consultancy services or resort to alternative mode of procurement in accordance with the 2016 RIRR of RA No. 9184; and b. refrain from engaging private consultants in the Municipality as legal consultants without the written concurrence of COA as prescribed by COA Circular No. 95-011.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality is now knowledgeable on the process of consultancy services procurement. No private lawyers were working as legal consultants in the Municipality as of the year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the: a. Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer to identify and categorize programs and projects that are pertinent to be properly charged to LDRRMF in accordance with CSC JMC No. 2014-1 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM JMC No. 2013-1; and to submit the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF to the Office of the Auditor on or before the 15th day after the end of each month for monitoring and evaluation; and b. Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF and to ensure that it is certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.
observations.managementAction: Expenditures charged to the LDRRMF for CY 2021 are now in compliance with the provisions of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-01 and NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1. The Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF is now regularly submitted to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period. The Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the DRRMF is now being reviewed and certified correct by the Municipal Accountant.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer to prepare and maintain separate RAAO for Quick Response Fund (QRF), Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) and Capital Outlay (CO) that will serve as a monitoring tool in determining the accuracy of the balances reported and submit the same to the Audit Team for review and evaluation.
observations.managementAction: RAAO for QRF, MOOE and CO are now being prepared and maintained by the Municipal Budget Officer.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to:
a. enforce the liquidation of cash advances strictly within the prescribed period and ensure that these are liquidated at the end of each year pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-002; and
b. send demand letters to the former Municipal Treasurer requiring the refund of her unliquidated cash advance amounting to P10,000.00.
observations.managementAction: A number of Cash Advances were still unliquidated as of year-end.
observations.reason: Cash Advances were still granted to officers and employees with outstanding cash advances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Budget Officer to justify in writing why the abovementioned expenditures were charged against the 20 per cent Development Fund; otherwise, revert to the 20 per cent Development Fund the cost incurred for the subject disbursements totaling P2,667,839.66.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the Management.
